_comment: Field-Consensus Threshold (FCT) registry. Migrated in-place from shared/organ-weight-thresholds.json (species-magnitude-thresholds-dog-nhp Phase A, M1: no legacy versioning). OM content is byte-equivalent to the pre-migration JSON. Severity classification reads this registry as single source of truth for per-(domain, endpoint, species, direction, sex) native-scale bands. Phase B (upcoming) migrates LB/BW/classify_severity call sites.

_schema.entries: Keyed by '<DOMAIN>.<endpoint_or_test_code>.<direction>'. DOMAIN is the SEND 2-char code (OM, LB, BW, VS, CV, MI, etc.). direction ∈ {up, down, both} — 'both' when severity depends on magnitude of change irrespective of sign (e.g., OM liver hypertrophy OR atrophy can be adverse).
_schema.entries.<key>.species_specific: True when thresholds vary by species. When false, use bands['any'].
_schema.entries.<key>.bands: Per-species (or 'any' when not species_specific) band thresholds. variation_ceiling = noise ceiling (below = trivial), concern_floor = mild-effect floor (optional; null for OM — migrated 3-tier data has no concern tier), adverse_floor = biologically meaningful floor, strong_adverse_floor = clearly adverse regardless of context.
_schema.entries.<key>.bands.<species>.any_signif: When true, any statistically significant change is treated as adverse regardless of magnitude (rodent brain/testes pattern). Numeric bands may still be present as documentation.
_schema.entries.<key>.coverage: full = all in-scope species have non-null bands; partial = some species missing/null; none = no numeric bands (classifier emits 'provisional' verdict).
_schema.entries.<key>.provenance: Entry-level provenance is the LOWEST (most-extrapolated) species-band provenance — honest-uncertainty default. Individual band provenance, when present, overrides for species-resolved lookup.
_schema.entries.<key>.threshold_reliability: Summary tier: high = published regulatory + industry validation; moderate = industry survey or single-source regulatory; low = extrapolated across species; speculative = proxy / stopping criterion.
_schema.joint_rules: Multi-endpoint bundles (e.g., Hy's Law L01, CRS Grade-2). Each carries provenance and a syndrome_rule_ref that must resolve against the live syndrome engine at load time (bidirectional integrity — AC-F2-1).
_schema.cv_reference: Documentation only — published CVs justifying the threshold choices. Not consumed by the severity classifier.

source_refs.citation: JMPR/EFSA threshold of toxicological concern (historical practice)
Hall AJ et al. 2012. Liver hypertrophy: a review of adaptive (adverse and non-adverse) changes — conclusions from the 3rd International ESTP Expert Workshop. Toxicol Pathol 40(7):971-994.
Choi T et al. 2011. Beagle dog historical control organ-weight CV data.

OM.LIVER.both.notes: Liver is the most-studied organ for adaptive vs adverse distinction. Hall 2012 defined the LB panel gate. Dog thresholds wider due to higher CVs (Choi 2011: M=15%, F=21%).
OM.LIVER.both.dog_adaptive_notes: ALP elevation without bilirubin elevation should not preclude adaptive classification in dogs (C-ALP confound). ALT is the primary liver injury discriminator.

source_refs.citation: JMPR/EFSA threshold of toxicological concern (historical practice)
Choi T et al. 2011. Beagle CV data.

OM.KIDNEY.both.notes: Kidney weight changes >=10% are concerning. Male rat a2u-globulin nephropathy is not human-relevant. Dog uses single wider female-driven threshold (Choi 2011: M CV=12%, F CV=18%); 15% ceiling is conservative for males but defensible.

source_refs.citation: Choi T et al. 2011. Beagle heart CV data (M=10%, F=13%).

OM.HEART.both.notes: Heart has low normal variation (CV ~5-8% rodent). Dog heart CV is higher (Choi 2011: M=10%, F=13%).

source_refs.citation: Choi T et al. 2011. Dog brain CV ~5%.

OM.BRAIN.both.notes: Brain weight is tightly regulated. Rat/mouse: any statistically significant change is biologically meaningful (any_significant policy). Dog brain CV ~5% (Choi 2011), so dog uses 5% floor instead of any_significant.

source_refs.citation: Choi T et al. 2011. Dog adrenal CV ~20%.

OM.ADRENAL.both.notes: Adrenal weight is highly variable, especially in mice and dogs. Species-specific thresholds prevent false positives. Dog adrenal CV ~20% (Choi 2011).

source_refs.citation: Capen CC 1997. Mechanistic data and risk assessment of selected toxic endpoints of the thyroid gland. Toxicol Pathol 25(1):39-48.
Choi T et al. 2011. Dog thyroid CV ~15%.

OM.THYROID.both.notes: Thyroid weight changes often secondary to liver enzyme induction (liver-thyroid axis). Capen 1997. Dog thyroid CV ~15% (Choi 2011).

source_refs.citation: Choi T et al. 2011. Dog spleen CV ~20%.
Amato DV et al. 2022. Historical control data for nonhuman primate organ weights — spleen Tier C qualitative.

OM.SPLEEN.both.notes: Spleen weight is naturally variable. Large changes may indicate immune response or stress. Dog spleen CV ~20% (Choi 2011). NHP: Amato 2022 Tier C qualitative — no numeric FCT, classifier emits provisional verdict.

source_refs.citation: Choi T et al. 2011. Dog thymus CV ~25%.
Amato DV et al. 2022. NHP thymus Tier C qualitative.

OM.THYMUS.both.notes: Thymus weight is age-sensitive and stress-responsive. Involution is normal in older animals. Dog thymus CV ~25% (Choi 2011). NHP: qualitative only.

source_refs.citation: Choi T et al. 2011. Dog testes CV ~15%.

OM.TESTES.both.notes: Testes weight is tightly regulated in rodents. Dog testes CV ~15% (Choi 2011). Changes >=15% are concerning for reproductive toxicity. NHP entry absent pending species calibration (GAP-SMT-07).

source_refs.citation: Choi T et al. 2011. Dog epididymis CV ~12%.

OM.EPIDIDYMIDES.both.notes: Epididymal weight changes often parallel testicular effects. Dog epididymis CV ~12% (Choi 2011).

source_refs.citation: Historical ovarian weight CV surveys (estrous cycle stage variability).

OM.OVARIES.both.notes: Ovarian weight is highly variable due to estrous cycle stage. Higher thresholds prevent false positives. No dog-specific threshold -- ANCOVA-only for dogs (CV >60%).

source_refs.citation: Historical uterine weight CV surveys (most variable organ weight).

OM.UTERUS.both.notes: Uterine weight is the most variable organ weight due to estrous cycle. Very high thresholds required. No dog-specific threshold -- ANCOVA-only for dogs (CV >60%).

source_refs.citation: Amato DV et al. 2022. NHP lungs Tier C qualitative (perfusion/inflation variability).

OM.LUNGS.both.notes: Lung weight changes may indicate edema, inflammation, or fibrosis. Residual blood can confound. NHP: perfusion/inflation variability makes quantitative screening unreliable.

source_refs.citation: Amato DV et al. 2022. NHP pancreas Tier C qualitative (colony CV ~27-30%).

OM.PANCREAS.both.notes: High variability organ (colony CV ~27-30% for NHP). Not always weighed in NHP tox studies. NHP: qualitative only.

hys_law._comment: Hy's Law: concurrent ALT + TBILI elevation is a strong signal of DILI. Maps to the L03 rule in shared/rules/lab-clinical-rules.json; the FCT registry carries the regulatory provenance + source refs, the LB rule engine evaluates the per-animal condition.

source_refs.citation: FDA Guidance for Industry: Drug-Induced Liver Injury -- Premarketing Clinical Evaluation (2009).
CIOMS Working Group. Drug-Induced Liver Injury (DILI) (2020).
